_comment: Library of fully-generated supporting characters referenced from story prompts (imagegen/prompts/<type>_<n>.json) as {TOKEN} placeholders. Maintained by the `character-config` skill. The input-photo person (the protagonist) is NEVER defined here -- only the conjured supporting cast. See README.md for the placeholder/runtime contract.

runtime.resolution: Strip the curly braces from a placeholder found in a prompt string, look up characters[<TOKEN>].description, and substitute it in. This is the same flat string-replace mechanism workflow.py already uses for USER_ID / STORY_ID. characters[*].description is the ONLY field read at runtime; everything else in this file is authoring metadata.
runtime.fallback: If a token has no entry under `characters`, compose a description on the fly from `dimensions` by parsing the GENDER_<g>_AGE_<a> prefix (see _recipe). The _RACE_<r> segment is OPTIONAL: include it (GENDER_F_AGE_70_RACE_ASIAN) to pin an exact race, or omit it (GENDER_F_AGE_70) to have a race drawn at RANDOM from `dimensions.race` for that job. The hair/build/wardrobe/features fragments are likewise picked at RANDOM (a fresh look per job, identical across that job's panels), filtered by `restrictions`: each dimension value lists under `avoid` the restriction groups it must skip, so a child never draws an adult-only fragment, a man never a feminine one, a non-binary character neither, etc. A trailing _<suffix> (GENDER_F_AGE_06_FRIEND2) is ignored at compose time -- it only keeps two otherwise-identical raceless tokens distinct. Enumerate the character explicitly under `characters` whenever you need a fixed look that stays byte-for-byte identical across every panel and every job.
runtime.example: {GENDER_F_AGE_70}  ->  random race + random look (per job);  {GENDER_M_AGE_30_RACE_ASIAN}  ->  East Asian + random look (race pinned, rest composed)

_recipe.comment: How a character's `description` is composed from the modular tables below. characters[*].description is the COMPILED output and must be regenerated whenever any fragment it references changes -- it is the single source of truth at runtime, the refs are bookkeeping.
rules: gender_noun: use dimensions.gender[g].noun_child when dimensions.age[a].child is true, otherwise dimensions.gender[g].noun.
Articles are baked into dimensions.age[a].phrase (e.g. 'an 8-year-old', 'a 30-year-old', 'an elderly').
The trailing ', with {features}' segment is omitted entirely when the character has no `features` ref.
Keep descriptions appearance-only (age, ethnicity, build, hair, clothing, fixed features). Never bake in actions, expressions, poses or positions -- those belong in the per-panel story prompt.

hair.PONYTAIL_BROWN: brown hair in a high ponytail
hair.GREY_BUN: grey hair tied in a low bun
hair.LOW_FADE_BLACK: black hair in a low fade
hair.MAN_BUN_DARK: dark hair tied in a man bun
hair.PUFFS_BLACK: black hair in two puff buns

build.AVERAGE_MEDIUM: an average build and medium height
build.SLIM_TALL: a slim build and tall stature

wardrobe.CASUAL_TEE_JEANS: a plain navy crew-neck t-shirt and dark blue jeans
wardrobe.HOODIE_GREEN: a forest-green hoodie and black joggers
wardrobe.DENIM_JACKET_TEE: a blue denim jacket over a white t-shirt
wardrobe.BUSINESS_GREY_SUIT: a charcoal-grey business suit with a white shirt
wardrobe.CASUAL_HOME_LILAC: a lilac knit jumper and comfortable beige trousers
wardrobe.CASUAL_HOME_BLUE: a light-blue checked shirt and grey chinos
wardrobe.SWEATER_BURGUNDY: a burgundy knit sweater and dark trousers
wardrobe.CARDIGAN_FLORAL: a soft beige cardigan over a floral blouse and a long grey skirt
wardrobe.BLOUSE_SKIRT_WHITE: a white blouse and a pleated navy skirt
wardrobe.SARI_BLUE: a deep blue sari with gold trim
wardrobe.FLANNEL_PLAID: a red plaid flannel shirt and blue jeans
wardrobe.POLO_KHAKI: a navy polo shirt and khaki chinos
wardrobe.SCHOOL_UNIFORM_BLUE: a navy school uniform with a white collared shirt
wardrobe.DUNGAREES_RED: red dungarees over a white t-shirt
wardrobe.TSHIRT_STRIPED: a striped orange t-shirt and blue shorts
wardrobe.TEE_TEAL_SHORTS: a teal t-shirt and grey shorts
wardrobe.RAINCOAT_RED_KID: a bright red raincoat and yellow boots
wardrobe.OVERALLS_BLUE_KID: blue denim overalls over a yellow t-shirt
wardrobe.DRESS_PINK_FLORAL: a pink dress with a small flower print

features.BEAUTY_MARK: a small beauty mark on one cheek

restrictions._comment: Fragment keys reserved to one demographic group, consumed by workflow.py's RANDOM fallback composition (NOT enumerated descriptions). When a GENDER_..._AGE_... token (with or without the optional _RACE_...) has no `characters` entry, the random hair/build/wardrobe/features draw drops the keys reserved to any group the character must `avoid` (see dimensions.gender[g].avoid / dimensions.age[a].avoid). Race carries no restrictions -- when _RACE_ is omitted a race is simply drawn at random from `dimensions.race`. Age is a THREE-way band, not binary: a child (06-16) avoids both `adult_only` and `elderly_only`; a middle adult (25-45) avoids `child_only` and `elderly_only`; only an elderly character (60, 70) may draw `elderly_only` looks (grey/thinning hair, smile lines) on top of `adult_only` — so a 25-year-old never rolls a grey bun. Gender: a man avoids `fem_only`, a woman `masc_only`, a non-binary character both. A key listed under no group suits everyone; a key may appear in several groups (e.g. a beard is masc_only AND adult_only; thinning grey hair is masc_only AND elderly_only). If every option in a table is filtered away, the restriction is ignored for that table rather than producing nothing. Keep these in sync when you add a fragment whose look is clearly age- or gender-specific.

characters._comment: Enumerated, fixed-look characters (TOKEN -> { refs, description }). Empty by design: every story currently relies on the RANDOM fallback (see runtime.fallback), so each job rolls a fresh race + look that stays consistent across that job's panels. Add an entry here only when you need a character whose look is byte-for-byte identical across every panel AND every job; an enumerated description wins over the random composition for that exact token.
